'''Jonny Hepworth''' (born 25 December 1982) is an English former [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer. He played club level rugby union (RU) for [[Leinster Rugby]], [[Clontarf FC]], [[Leeds Carnegie|Leeds Tykes]], and, [[Rotherham R.U.F.C.]], and club level rugby league (RL) for [[National Conference League|Castleford Panthers ARLFC]], [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]], [[Castleford Tigers]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]].

He was born in [[Castleford]]. He currently plays for [[Featherstone Rovers]]. Hepworth previously played for the [[Leeds Carnegie|Leeds Tykes]], making his début for them at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley Carnegie Stadium]] against [[London Welsh]] on 3 September 2006 at the start of the 2006–07 [[National League 1]] season.

==Rugby league==
===Leeds Rhinos===
Hepworth played junior [[rugby league]] for the [[National Conference League|Castleford Panthers ARLFC]] before joining [[Super League]] outfit [[Leeds Rhinos]].

Hepworth [[Captain (sports)|captained]] the [[Leeds Rhinos]] Academy team when they beat local rivals the [[Bradford Bulls]] U-19 side in the Senior Academy Championship with a 12–7 victory in a [[Grand final|Grand Final]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]] in October 2002.

He made only one appearance for the Rhinos, under coach [[Daryl Powell]], before briefly joining the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] on loan in 2002 and his hometown club the [[Castleford Tigers]], also on loan, in 2003.<ref>{{cite web |title=Cullen and Steadman handed Origin Match honours |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/cullen-and-steadman-handed-origin-match-honours-104717.html |website=The Independent |access-date=20 February 2024 |date=15 May 2003}}</ref> Broncos coach [[Tony Rea]] said on the arrival of Hepworth in May 2002: ''"He's an exciting young player and will give us some nice back-up."''
In 2011 he signed for [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] side [[Featherstone Rovers]] becoming a key figure in their side.

===Castleford Tigers===
Hepworth's move to Castleford Tigers was made permanent in July 2003, signing a three-year contract.<ref>{{cite web |title=Orr and agent fear rumble at the Jungle |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2003/jul/11/rugbyleague.castleford |website=The Guardian |access-date=20 February 2024 |date=11 July 2003}}</ref> Castleford were relegated from the [[Super League]] in 2004, but Hepworth helped them re-gain promotion, and played in the [[2005 Rugby League National Leagues|2005 National Leagues One]] Grand Final victory against [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]].<ref>{{cite web |last1=Wilson |first1=Andy |title=Brilliant Davis survives rough treatment to put Castleford back on the big stage |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2005/oct/10/rugbyleague.sport |website=The Guardian |access-date=20 February 2024 |date=10 October 2005}}</ref>

In September 2005 he switched codes and signed for [[Pro14|Celtic League]] side [[Leinster Rugby|Leinster]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Leinster announce two new signings |url=https://www.irishexaminer.com/sport/rugby/arid-30221865.html |website=Irish Examiner |access-date=20 February 2024 |date=21 September 2005}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
Hepworth returned to rugby league with [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] side [[Featherstone Rovers]] in 2011. He retired in 2013 due to injuries.<ref>{{cite web |title=Hepworth forced to reitre |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hepworth-forced-to-reitre |website=Love Rugby League |access-date=20 February 2024 |date=19 December 2013}}</ref>

==Rugby union==
Before making his full début for Leinster against [[Edinburgh Rugby|Edinburgh]] in Dublin on 6 November 2005 he said: '' "Apart from playing for a local team in [[Wakefield]] when I was 15, coming on against [[Bath Rugby|Bath]] was my first competitive game. But I think I've adapted well and fitted in well here."''

''"I'm picking up bits as we go along and settling in well, hopefully I can make a full début today and show what I can do."''

''"If I was switching to be a union forward it'd be difficult, but as a utility back in league you can fit in more quickly. In league there's not much kicking, it's mainly just torpedo kicks when you're punting."''

''"But here everyone can kick and our coach [[David Knox (rugby union)|David Knox]] has been helping me out with that."''

He also featured for [[Clontarf FC|Clontarf]] in [[Northside, Dublin|North Dublin]]. He then signed for the [[Leeds Carnegie|Leeds Tykes]], joining up again with [[Daryl Powell]], the Leeds Tykes' first team coach.

Hepworth joined the [[Rotherham R.U.F.C.]] for the [[2010–11 RFU Championship]], and made an immediate impression with his pace in possession and tenacious defence.
